claim: The ~3x Hand-A-vs-Stolfi suffix dealer density gap (Hand A 2.4182 vs Stolfi 0.80 from H-BV-EXTERNAL-PREDICTIONS-01 T2) has one or more of three non-exclusive sources: (a) Hand A has a larger Layer-1 inner inventory than Hand B and than Stolfi's reference (real inventory richness); (b) Brain-V's operationalisation of Stolfi's crust-mantle-core classification drifts from Stolfi's original (methodological artefact); (c) Hand A is more inflectionally productive than Hand B at the per-token level (genuine A/B productivity difference). H-BV-COMPOSITE-OUTER-01 ruled out Layer-2 composite decomposition as the driver (M4 FAIL, contribution 0.02 on a 1.62 excess). This hypothesis discriminates among the remaining candidates.
evidence_for: H-BV-COMPOSITE-OUTER-01 M4 failed at 0.2440 reduction; the actual composite-decomposition contribution was only 0.02 out of 1.62 excess. The 3x density gap is therefore NOT explained by Layer-2 composite stacking and requires a different explanation.
Hand A and Hand B are known to differ statistically (Currier 1976) — so any per-hand productivity difference (candidate c) has prior evidential support at the descriptive level.

Brain-V's Stolfi operationalisation in H-BV-EXTERNAL-PREDICTIONS-01 was implemented from Stolfi's category set but has never been validated against his original Hand B measurements. Without that validation, candidate (b) cannot be ruled out.

H-BV-SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01 showed the top-10 Hand A inner inventory covers 93% — a tighter concentration than a richer-inventory hypothesis would predict at first glance. But 'inventory size' to reach 80% coverage (Brain-V's standard measure) has not been computed for Hand B or compared to Stolfi's crust cardinality. Candidate (a) remains open until measured.
evidence_against: All three candidates can hold simultaneously. The test design intentionally allows multiple passes rather than forcing a single winner, so there is no false-exclusivity risk. But this also means the test cannot adjudicate among them by exclusion — a ranking would require effect-size comparison.

The Stolfi reference for candidate (a) is approximate. Stolfi's published crust cardinality is 14-15 glyphs; his work does not report inventory_size_80 under Brain-V's operational definition. Using 15 as the reference is a reasonable proxy but not a direct measurement.

Candidate (b) is tested by comparing Brain-V's Hand B measurement to Stolfi's published 0.80. If Brain-V's Hand B suffix dealer mean is far from 0.80, operationalisation drift is established — but the direction and magnitude of drift still need interpretation (is it tokenisation, category assignment, or something else?). A passed (b) is a starting point, not a conclusion.

Sample-size effects differ between hands. Hand A has ~11,000 tokens; Hand B has more (Currier B is the larger partition). Inventory-size measurements will have different bootstrap variance in each hand — the p<0.01 threshold for (a) needs enough resamples to be meaningful.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  DATA: Voynich corpus from raw/perception/voynich-corpus.json. Partition by currier_language field. Hand A = folios with currier_language == 'A'; Hand B = folios with currier_language == 'B'. Hands with other values (neutral, mixed) excluded from all measurements.

  TOKENISATION (LOCKED, Stolfi-consistent):
    MULTI = ['cth', 'cph', 'ckh', 'cfh', 'ch', 'sh', 'ee'], longest-match-first.
    NOTE: 'ol' is NOT treated as a ligature in this test, matching the tokenisation of H-BV-EXTERNAL-PREDICTIONS-01 T2 so the 2.4182 baseline carries forward cleanly. Rationale: we are diagnosing a number measured under that tokenisation; changing tokenisations here would compound the methodology drift being diagnosed.

  CATEGORIES (LOCKED per Stolfi 2000):
    CORE_SET  = {t, p, k, f, cth, cph, ckh, cfh}
    MANTLE_SET = {ch, sh, ee}
    CRUST_SET = {d, l, r, s, n, x, i, m, g, q, o, y, a, e}

  THREE MEASUREMENTS (M1, M2, M3) and three pass/fail DECISIONS ((a), (b), (c)).

  M1 — LAYER-1 INNER INVENTORY SIZE (80% coverage) per hand:
    For hand H in {A, B}:
      Take all H tokens tokenised per MULTI; keep only those with length(t) >= 3 glyph-units.
      For each such token, the 'inner' glyph is position length(t) - 2 (second-from-last).
      Count frequency of each distinct inner glyph.
      Rank inner glyphs by frequency descending.
      inventory_size_80(H) = smallest K such that sum of top-K frequencies / total inner occurrences >= 0.80.

    BOOTSTRAP for (a) significance:
      Resample tokens with replacement 1000 times, compute inventory_size_80 on each resample.
      Record bootstrap distributions D_A and D_B.
      p_a = fraction of resamples where inventory_size_80_A_sample <= inventory_size_80_B_sample (one-sided; we expect A > B under (a)).

    STOLFI REFERENCE (FIXED, NOT MEASURED):
      inventory_size_80_stolfi_proxy = 15
      Rationale: Stolfi 2000 reports ~14-15 glyph types dominating the crust layer of his primary (Currier B) corpus. 15 is taken as the comparative baseline.

    REPORT: inventory_size_80(A), inventory_size_80(B), bootstrap means and 95% CIs for each, p_a, Stolfi reference 15.

  M2 — MEAN TOKENISED LENGTH per hand (proxy for 'morphemes per token'):
    For hand H in {A, B}:
      mean_length(H) = mean over all H tokens of length(tokenise(t)) in glyph-units.
      std_length(H)  = standard deviation.
      n(H)           = number of tokens.
    Welch's t-test on per-token lengths: A vs B. Record t-statistic and p-value.
    REPORT: mean_length(A), mean_length(B), t-statistic, p-value.
    INTERPRETATION NOTE: This is a proxy for morpheme count. A true morpheme count would require a morphological parser. Glyph-unit length after ligature-aware tokenisation is the closest practical surrogate and is what Stolfi himself uses in his published analyses.

  M3 — STOLFI-CONSISTENT SUFFIX DEALER MEAN per hand:
    For hand H in {A, B}:
      Take H tokens, tokenised per MULTI, excluding q-initial tokens AND tokens without at least one CORE glyph.
      For each qualifying token t: split at first CORE glyph index, let suffix = glyphs after that index.
      per_token_suffix_dealer = count of glyphs in suffix that are in CRUST_SET.
      suffix_dealer_mean(H) = mean of per_token_suffix_dealer across qualifying H tokens.
      suffix_dealer_std(H)  = standard deviation.
      n_split(H)            = number of qualifying tokens.
    Welch's t-test on per-token suffix dealer counts: A vs B. Record t-statistic and p-value.
    REPORT: suffix_dealer_mean(A), suffix_dealer_mean(B), t-statistic, p-value.
    SANITY CHECK: suffix_dealer_mean(A) should be ~2.4182 (the EXTERNAL-PREDICTIONS-01 T2 value) to within 0.01. Deviation >0.01 indicates a tokenisation or category drift in this script vs. the earlier one; flag and investigate before interpreting (b).

  DECISION RULES (LOCKED, NON-EXCLUSIVE):

    (a) INVENTORY-RICHNESS PASS iff:
          inventory_size_80(A) > inventory_size_80(B)
          AND p_a < 0.01
          AND inventory_size_80(A) > 15 (Stolfi reference)

    (b) OPERATIONALISATION-ARTEFACT PASS iff:
          (suffix_dealer_mean(B) - 0.80) / (suffix_dealer_mean(A) - 0.80) > 0.50
          (equivalently, suffix_dealer_mean(B) > 0.80 + 0.50 * (suffix_dealer_mean(A) - 0.80))
          with suffix_dealer_mean(A) expected near 2.4182, this threshold is suffix_dealer_mean(B) > ~1.61

    (c) A-OVER-B-PRODUCTIVITY PASS iff:
          Welch t-test p-value (M3) < 0.01
          AND (suffix_dealer_mean(A) - suffix_dealer_mean(B)) / (suffix_dealer_mean(A) - 0.80) > 0.30
          (at least 30% of the Hand-A excess over Stolfi is attributable to A > B, not shared methodology)

    MULTIPLE PASSES ALLOWED. Report all three verdicts independently. If none pass: flag as 'UNDIAGNOSED' — the 3x gap is not explained by the three candidates; a follow-up hypothesis is needed.

  WORKED EXAMPLE (to lock M1 denominator):
    Token 'chedy' tokenises under MULTI as ['ch', 'e', 'd', 'y']. Length 4. Inner glyph = position 4-2 = 2, which is 'd'. Contributes:
      count_inner_occurrences['d'] += 1
      total_inner_occurrences += 1
    Token 'ky' tokenises as ['k', 'y']. Length 2. Excluded (length < 3).
    Token 'daiin' tokenises as ['d', 'a', 'i', 'i', 'n']. Length 5. Inner glyph = position 3, which is 'i'. Contributes:
      count_inner_occurrences['i'] += 1
      total_inner_occurrences += 1
    inventory_size_80 = smallest K such that sum(top-K counts) / total_inner_occurrences >= 0.80

  WORKED EXAMPLE (to lock M3 denominator):
    Token 'chedy' = ['ch', 'e', 'd', 'y']. No CORE glyph (t, p, k, f, cth, cph, ckh, cfh). Excluded from M3.
    Token 'qokedy' = ['q', 'o', 'k', 'e', 'd', 'y']. q-initial. Excluded from M3.
    Token 'chekedy' = ['ch', 'e', 'k', 'e', 'd', 'y']. Not q-initial, contains 'k' (CORE). Split at first CORE:
      prefix = ['ch', 'e']
      core   = 'k'
      suffix = ['e', 'd', 'y']
    per_token_suffix_dealer = count of {e, d, y} that are in CRUST = 3.
test_metric: M1 inventory_size_80 per hand with bootstrap; M2 mean tokenised length per hand with Welch t-test; M3 Stolfi-consistent suffix dealer mean per hand with Welch t-test.
test_threshold: (a) inv_A > inv_B at p_a<0.01 AND inv_A > 15; (b) (B_mean - 0.80)/(A_mean - 0.80) > 0.50; (c) t-test p<0.01 AND (A_mean - B_mean)/(A_mean - 0.80) > 0.30. Non-exclusive. Report all passes.
tests_remaining: locked_density_diagnosis_test: run M1/M2/M3 on Hand A and Hand B per locked procedure; evaluate (a)/(b)/(c) per locked thresholds; report all passes, all failures, and sanity-check result for Hand A suffix dealer mean vs 2.4182
reasoning: H-BV-COMPOSITE-OUTER-01 M4 ruled out Layer-2 composite decomposition as the driver of Hand A's 3x suffix dealer density excess. The remaining plausible sources are: (a) Hand A has a genuinely richer Layer-1 inner inventory, (b) Brain-V's Stolfi operationalisation drifts from Stolfi's own, or (c) Hand A is more inflectionally productive than Hand B. These are not mutually exclusive, and the test is designed to report all that hold. The Hand B suffix dealer measurement under Brain-V's operationalisation is the key diagnostic: if it matches Stolfi's 0.80, the operationalisation is faithful and (b) fails; if it's elevated, (b) has support. The A-vs-B comparison via Welch's t-test operationalises (c). The inventory-size-80 bootstrap on top-rank glyphs operationalises (a) against both Hand B and a fixed Stolfi proxy of 15. Every metric has a worked example following the T3 and M4 methodology lessons. Tokenisation is locked to the EXTERNAL-PREDICTIONS-01 convention (no 'ol' ligature) so the 2.4182 baseline carries forward cleanly — changing tokenisations would compound the drift being diagnosed.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-17 user-directed follow-up to H-BV-COMPOSITE-OUTER-01 PARTIAL_DENSITY verdict. The central question is: if not composite decomposition, what does explain the 3x excess?
